title: dichroa cyanea
intro: Dichroa cyanea, also known as the medicinal dichroa or Chinese foxglove, is a species of flowering plant in the family Saxifragaceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and removing toxins from the body. The plant's roots and rhizomes are rich in iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and supporting immune function. Dichroa cyanea is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its medicinal properties can also be extracted through infusion or tincture preparations.
